title: Global Industrialization
description: This exercise will cover the start and spread of industrialization through the themes of ENV and TEC. Each of these themes will have a type and each type will scale 5 items, for a total of 12.
topic_path: The Industrial Revolution Spreads in the Period from 1750 to 1900 CE | 5.4

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following environmental factors most contributed to the rise of industrialization in Britain from 1750 to 1900 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The British Isles are relatively flat, allowing for roads and canals to be built.
The British Isles are mountainous, allowing for a high number of coal mines.
The British Isles are made up of islands, which had sedentary deposits of silt to fuel factories.
The British Isles are spacious, which led to a large density of cotton plantations.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best summarizes the relationship between colonization and industrialization during the industrial era from 1750 to 1900 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Raw materials brought in from colonies supplied industry and fed people.
Colonies provided captive markets for goods manufactured in each colony.
Foreign ports allowed for the trade of manufactured goods from Asia.
Militaries honed in battle abroad provided protection for young industries.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**The events shown in the image above are best understood in the context of which of the following broader processes in the period 1750 to 1900 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Print of a large building with wealthy-looking people, horses, and carriages loitering outside.
options.caption: View from the Knightsbridge Road of The Crystal Palace in Hyde Park for the Grand International Exhibition of 1851. Dedicated to the Royal Commissioners., London: Read & Co. Engravers & Printers, 1851. Source: Public Domain

choices.content: the emergence of Great Britain as the global leader in manufacturing
the establishment of the world’s first colonies overseas
the development of new techniques in agriculture such as crop rotation
the end of the Protestant Reformation which brought on years of peace in Great Britain

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Industrial Revolution is generally accepted by historians as beginning in Great Britain, even though there were also factors in China that could have supported industrialization. Which of the following best summarizes the difference between environmental factors in China and Great Britain that led to industrialization from 1750 to 1900 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the proximity of coal to economic centers
access to natural resources
the proximity of economic centers to the sea
prominent systems of canals and rivers

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains a demographic possibility for why China did not industrialize before Great Britain from 1750 to 1900 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: China’s large labor supply did not necessitate machinery.
China’s mountainous interior made it difficult to move coal to economic centers.
China had a large population of landed elites who were not interested in economic change.
China had a disproportionately old population who could not work in factories.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes a way in which industrialization has shaped the environment?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The use of fossil fuels exceeds the time it takes for them to replenish.
The use of fossil fuels created a decline in the human population.
The rise in machinery led to riskier, more dangerous jobs.
The adoption of technology  hindered the production of food.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains the rise of wheat crops in Russia in the time period from 1750 to 1900 CE?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the British need for cheap bread
the spread of new agricultural techniques
the Little Ice Age subsided
the implementation of wage labor

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an illustration of how locations of production have changed over time?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Copper production became centered in Wales.
Textile production became centered in India.
Silk production increased in Africa.
Cotton production increased in the Mediterranean.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Industrial Revolution negatively impacted free people in the Caribbean. Which of the following changes in production most caused this effect?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Europeans took the sugar business to Asia, devastating the sugar economy in the Caribbean.
Europeans began producing their own cotton, lowering the value of imported cotton from the Caribbean.
New mechanized systems of agriculture made it impossible for smaller farmers in the Caribbean to compete.
The introduction of new diseases in the Caribbean brought over on European steam ships harmed the local population.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on your knowledge of world history and the image above, which of the following depicts the broad historic context for the decline of the Mughal Empire?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Map of India that shows an increase in land under the control of the British East India Company.
options.caption:
Source: Image of map of India under the British East India Company, comparing 1837 with 1857.
Public domain.

choices.content: the decline in manufactured goods from Asia
the rise in the production of silk in India
the introduction of new diseases to the native population
the decline of the Indian Ocean Trade Network

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Based on your knowledge of world history and the image above, what role did commercial contracts play in the de-industrialization of India?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Map of India that shows an increase in land under the control of the British East India Company.
options.caption:
Image of map of India under the British East India Company, comparing 1837 with 1857
Source: Public domain

choices.content: They gave Europeans a foothold to increase economic and political control.
They turned over complete control to European joint-stock companies.
They increased the number of manufactured goods that could be exported from Indian ports.
They spread European influence for the betterment of indigenous Indians along the coast.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the best example of how textile production in Britain impacted India from 1750 to 1900 CE?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: wide-spread famines and mass migrations
increased silk production and cotton exports
mass immigration to Britain from India
decline in raw cotton production
